title: Puffed Venezuelan Plantains Pastry
description: A delicious and unique pastry dish from Venezuela, featuring roasted plantains and a dairy-free, aerated dough.
instructions: Peel the plantains and slice into 1-inch pieces.
In a pan, heat 2 tbsp of coconut oil and sauté the plantains until lightly browned.
In a separate bowl, whisk together almond milk, apple cider vinegar, dill, and salt.
Add the yeast to the bowl and let it sit for 5 minutes until frothy.
Add the flour, sugar, and yeast mixture to the bowl and mix until a dough forms.
Knead the dough for 5 minutes until it becomes smooth and elastic.
Place the dough in a greased bowl, cover it with a damp cloth and let it rise for 1 hour.
Punch down the dough and roll it out into a 1/4-inch thick sheet.
Place the sheet of dough in a greased baking dish and top with the roasted plantains.
Bake the pastry for 25-30 minutes until golden brown.
Remove from the oven and let it cool for 10 minutes before serving.
